plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL entirely to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% to favor income and broad index exposure.
Trim QQQ to 10% to reduce broad index and meet the mandate.
Buy AAPL at 5% to add a top-quality name from the scanner.
This plan aims for capital preservation with a large cash buffer.
conservative.read: This conservative approach targets around 1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces overall risk by eliminating leveraged ETFs and diversifying holdings. Expect lower potential drawdowns but also more modest returns.

moves.note: Reduce broad index, scanner flags trim

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim QQQI to 10% as the scanner flags a sell, and QQQ to 10% due to trim flag.
Buy AVGO at 10% and AAPL at 10% to add quality tech from strong scanner setups.
This plan balances risk and growth, keeping a small cash buffer.
Total adds are kept within total trims plus buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced approach aims for around 2.5% per month, prioritizing risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration while blending broad index with quality tech. Expect moderate returns with controlled drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell QQQI and SOXL, as they are flagged for sell by the scanner.
Trim QQQ to 10% due to the scanner's trim flag, reallocating capital.
Increase SMH to 15% to lean into existing semiconductor exposure.
Buy LEU at 15% and AVGO at 15% as they are the highest-scoring scanner setups.
This plan uses a smaller cash buffer and concentrates in strong setups.
aggressive.read: This aggressive approach targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretched growth. It leans into higher-scoring setups and allows for concentration, but also carries significantly higher risk and potential for larger drawdowns. Leveraged ETFs decay daily, and strict stop discipline is crucial to manage losses.

moves.note: Reduce broad index, scanner flags trim

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL entirely to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize exposure to income-generating ETFs.
Trim QQQ to 5% due to scanner flag and to reduce growth exposure.
Sell SMH to reduce high-beta single name exposure.
This plan prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income over growth.
income.read: This income approach aims for around 1% per month, focusing on parking cash for yield. It rotates heavily into income/covered-call ETFs and broad index, while cutting high-beta and leveraged names. Distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff, so this is not a savings account but aims for steady, lower-volatility income.
